premiumSeo.metaDescription: How the {tool} works, who it is for, what to enter, and how to read the result. Pricing and margin-risk decision support from SectorCalc.
premiumSeo.h1: {tool}: price the job and protect the margin
premiumSeo.intro: The {tool} helps you pressure-test a job before you commit to a price. It turns your costs, quantities, and rates into a clear margin and risk read, so you can spot the hidden drivers that quietly erode profit and decide with confidence instead of guessing on the bid.
premiumSeo.howTo: Enter {inputs} for your {sector} scenario. SectorCalc runs governed, deterministic formulas and returns {outputs}. Add optional fields when you have them to sharpen threshold and risk reads.
premiumSeo.method: The {tool} uses SectorCalc's formula governance layer: validated inputs, a deterministic calculation pipeline, and threshold checks. It is a technical simulation — not financial, legal, or engineering advice.
premiumSeo.mistakes: Stale labor or material rates, skipped optional inputs that capture hidden loss, mixed units, and treating the read as guaranteed profit are frequent errors. {pain} Verify inputs before business decisions.
premiumSeo.problem: Most jobs lose margin long before the invoice, when setup, rework, overruns, and underpriced line items are never added up before the quote goes out. The {tool} makes those drivers visible so they stop surprising you.
premiumSeo.help: It applies SectorCalc's deterministic calculation engine to your inputs, then highlights the factors that move the result the most. You get a structured read you can defend, not a black-box number.
premiumSeo.who: Operators, estimators, owners, and managers who price work, review bids, or need a consistent baseline across a team will get the most from the {tool}.
premiumSeo.inputs: Core fields include {inputs} ({inputCount} inputs in total). Required fields stay minimal; optional ones refine the verdict when your data is ready.
premiumSeo.outputs: {tool} returns {outputs} with metrics, threshold alerts, and risk drivers — so you see why the number lands where it does and what to adjust.
premiumSeo.trust: Premium results can include a Calculation Summary of inputs, assumptions, and validation — saved as exportable decision summaries for internal review.

faq.a: You can preview the approach for free; full verdict, metrics, and premium decision summary are included in SectorCalc Pro.
No. It is a technical simulation and decision-support tool, not financial, legal, or engineering advice. Verify results before business decisions.
Results reflect the inputs you provide and SectorCalc's deterministic engine. Better inputs produce a sharper, more defensible read.
Yes. Premium results can be exported as decision summaries with a Calculation Summary of how the numbers were derived.
It is tuned for its sector's pricing and margin patterns, and it sits alongside the rest of SectorCalc's 27-sector toolset.
Open the analyzer, enter {inputs}, and review {outputs}. Adjust inputs to stress-test the scenario before you commit to a price.

homeAbout.title: About Us
homeAbout.paragraph1: Profit often leaks from businesses through unnoticed costs, not sales. Incorrect pricing, calculation errors, waste, efficiency loss, and weak quoting discipline silently erode margins over time.
homeAbout.paragraph2: SectorCalc is engineered to make these invisible losses quantifiable. It elevates cost, proposal, efficiency, and risk decisions onto a faster, more transparent, and reliable baseline.
homeAbout.beliefSubtitle: Precision in calculation is the foundation of reliable performance.
homeAbout.highlightPrimary: A platform to expose hidden losses,
homeAbout.highlightSecondary: and transform computation into reliable decision support.

aboutPage.seoTitle: About SectorCalc | Industrial Calculation & Decision Support
aboutPage.seoDescription: SectorCalc helps businesses expose hidden losses, quantify operational risks, and turn industrial calculations into reliable decision support.

hero.title: Computation, turned into reliable decisions.
hero.lead: SectorCalc is a global industrial calculation and decision support platform that helps businesses expose hidden losses and act on them with confidence.

why.title: Why SectorCalc exists
why.body: Most operational losses never appear on an invoice. They hide in pricing, scrap, setup time, efficiency drops, and weak quoting discipline, and they erode margins quietly for years.

what.title: What SectorCalc is
what.body: A focused suite of sector calculators and decision reports that turn raw operational inputs into clear, defensible numbers, not another spreadsheet or a heavyweight ERP.

problem.title: The problem we solve
problem.body: Teams make cost, quote, and risk decisions on incomplete information. SectorCalc makes the invisible measurable, so decisions rest on evidence instead of guesswork.

how.title: How our system works
how.body: You provide the inputs you know; the system resolves what is required, runs governed deterministic calculations, and validates every result against invariants and benchmarks before showing it.

different.title: What makes us different
different.body: Every result carries a transparent calculation summary, indicative regional benchmarks, and documented assumptions you can review with your team before acting.

who.title: Who it is built for
who.body: Owner-operators, estimators, field teams, and consultants who need fast, reliable answers without hiring a full pricing desk or deploying enterprise software.

building.title: What we are building
building.body: A global standard for sector-specific calculation intelligence, expanding across industries while keeping every formula governed, auditable, and trustworthy.

manifesto.primary: A platform to expose hidden losses,
manifesto.secondary: and transform computation into reliable decision support.

hero.title: Help build verified sector benchmarks

who.title: Who should apply
who.item1: Operators tracking scrap, setup, route, energy or margin loss
who.item3: Small teams that need clear calculations before adopting complex software
who.item4: Teams willing to give structured feedback on calculator fit

data.title: What data we collect
data.item1: Industry, company size and main loss area

benefits.title: What you get

benefits.item2: Direct input into sector benchmark design
benefits.item3: Optional case study participation with explicit permission

form.heading: Apply for beta partner access

sectorFooter.tagline: Sector calculators for production, industry and business decisions.
sectorFooter.noteBody: Calculators are for preliminary review and decision support. Verify with your own data before critical commercial decisions.

calculatorLibrary.metaDescription: Public calculator catalog resources for SectorCalc: LLMs index, sector tool listings, and catalog entry points.
calculatorLibrary.title: Calculator Library
calculatorLibrary.lead: Machine-readable indexes and catalog entry points for SectorCalc sector calculators.

shape.title: Input guide
shape.description: This guide maps measurement fields to their physical counterpart on the object.

costBreakdown.description: This guide visually maps cost drivers to quote price and safe margin impact.

processFlow.description: This guide shows how operational inputs convert into process loss and monetary impact.

quoteRisk.description: This guide links machine time, OEE drivers and quote inputs to minimum safe price risk.

inputGuide.title: Input guide
inputGuide.description: This diagram shows which measurement or value each input field refers to.

standard.title: Usage agreement
standard.body: This calculation provides a preliminary assessment based on your inputs and deterministic methodology. Results do not replace quotes, investment, engineering, tax, legal, or safety decisions. For critical decisions, verify with your own data, qualified experts, and applicable regulations.

elevated.title: Important usage notice
elevated.body: This tool models commercial or compliance-sensitive exposure. Outputs are indicative only and must be validated against your contracts, payroll records, and local rules before action.

engineering.title: Engineering reference notice
engineering.body: This tool provides standard-referenced pre-calculation only. Final design, safety, and conformity decisions require authorized engineering review.

standards.selectorNote: Default follows region; you may switch reference systems for comparison.
standards.engineeringWarning: This tool provides standard-referenced pre-calculation only. Final design, safety, and conformity decisions require authorized engineering review.

hero.headline: Stop letting your numbers
hero.headlineEm: discover your losses before you do.
hero.subtitle: Most businesses track revenue. Few quantify operational losses. SectorCalc shows what you're losing — even before you know which calculation to run.
hero.micro: No credit card · No Excel · Results in 60 seconds

pain.ticker: While you read this page, operational losses are accumulating in manufacturing facilities across Europe due to incorrectly calculated scrap rates.
pain.label: Where Is The Problem?
pain.title:
Businesses Don't Lose Money
Where They Think They Do.

cards.label: Scrap & Waste
cards.title: Most producers think 8%, real average is 14%.
cards.body: This 6-point gap creates €40,000–80,000 annual difference in a mid-sized plant. And it's invisible on the P&L.

cards.label: Machine Productivity
cards.title: OEE looks like 70%. The real cost impact is uncalculated.
cards.body: Without converting downtime, speed loss and quality rejects into cash, no optimization decision can be made.

cards.label: Pricing Errors
cards.title: Few calculate machine hour cost correctly when quoting.
cards.body: Machine hour rate calculated without VDI 2067, ASHRAE and ISO standards means margin lost on every quote.

cards.label: Energy Cost
cards.title: Energy bills are paid, specific consumption is not analyzed.
cards.body: Without per-unit, per-process-step energy cost, any sustainability claim remains an intention, not a fact.

calc.label: Calculate Now
calc.title: See Your Own Loss.
calc.subtitle: 3 inputs. 60 seconds. See your annual scrap cost and recovery potential instantly.
calc.input2Tip: Most firms estimate 8%, actual measurement shows 12–15%
calc.input3Tip: Full cost including material + labor + energy
calc.aiText: {rate}% scrap rate is above the industry average of 6–8%. This deviation alone indicates approximately {monthly} monthly operational loss.
calc.aiBlurText: The three most critical intervention points to reduce scrap by 2% in similar-scale facilities are: (1) Process parameter optimization — especially mold temperature tolerances. (2) Operator error analysis — shift calibration procedures. (3) Material acceptance criteria — incoming quality control thresholds.

social.label: Real Results
social.title: Not Numbers. Decisions.

Raw material waste rate was not visible. Annual loss magnitude was unknown.

methodology.title: No black boxes. Every formula cited.
methodology.lead: Engineering tools that engineers can trust. Sources shown, assumptions stated, edge cases documented.
methodology.card1Desc: Every calculator cites its source. ASME UG-27, ISO 5349, NIOSH lifting equation — no invented formulas, no guesswork.
methodology.card2Desc: Each tool links to its full derivation, guard conditions and known limitations. You know exactly what you're computing.
methodology.card3Desc: Results are decision-support, not sign-off. Every tool states its scope clearly. No false precision.
methodology.card4Desc: All calculations run in your browser. No input data leaves your device. No tracking of engineering data.

who.title: Who we are
who.body: SectorCalc is an Industrial Micro-SaaS App Store for operators who need measurement, loss detection, and decision-ready reports without ERP complexity or consultant retainers.
bullets: Built for shop floors, field crews, and owner-operators
Sector-specific calculators with governed formula contracts
Browser-first tools with optional saved reports for subscribers

what.title: What we do
what.body: We turn sector pain into structured calculation workflows: measure inputs, surface hidden loss, suggest action, and attach a calculation summary to results.
bullets: Free checks for quick exposure signals
Premium analyzers for verdict-grade outputs
Export-ready decision reports with validation metadata

for-whom.title: Who it is for
for-whom.body: Welding shops, CNC job shops, HVAC contractors, cleaning operators, restaurants, e-commerce sellers, and other sector operators who quote, bid, or plan with tight margins.
bullets: Teams without dedicated pricing engineers
Owners comparing quotes before signing
Consultants needing repeatable sector templates

replaces.title: What we replace (and what we do not)
replaces.body: SectorCalc replaces spreadsheet guesswork, notebook shortcuts, and random Google calculators — not SAP, Siemens, Oracle, or licensed engineering sign-off.
bullets: Not a quote-only Word template generator
Not an ERP or MES replacement
Not a certified compliance authority
Does replace ad-hoc Excel and opaque margin leaks

losses.title: Losses we make visible
losses.body: Each sector pack targets measurable loss families: scrap, setup time, callback risk, route deadhead, food waste, return erosion, energy demand charges, and similar operating leaks.
bullets: Material and labor exposure
Schedule and rework buffers
Margin leak drivers with suggested actions
Representative scenarios — not guaranteed savings

dual-intelligence.title: Dual-Intelligence methodology
dual-intelligence.body: Mind 2 (Requirement Engine) resolves which inputs are missing, derivable, or blocked before calculation. Mind 1 (Validation Engine) checks dimensions, invariants, and oracle/scenario coverage after calculation. The LLM layer is interface-only — never the calculation authority.
bullets: Contract-driven input requirements
Deterministic formula paths in production
Oracle and scenario audits in governance pipeline
Blocked results when validation fails

trust-trace.title: Calculation Summary
trust-trace.body: Calculation Summary shows canonical inputs, assumptions, formula contract reference, validation status, and coverage notes alongside results — so operators can see why a number appeared, not just the headline.
bullets: Canonical input list with rejected keys
Mind 2 pre-calc and Mind 1 post-calc summaries
Export payload for PDF decision summaries (data layer)
Governance metadata for saved premium results

responsibility.title: Responsibility boundary
responsibility.body: SectorCalc outputs are technical decision-support simulations based on user-provided inputs and documented assumptions. They are not legal, financial, tax, or engineering advice. Verify all outputs before bids, contracts, or operational commitments.
bullets: User-provided inputs drive every result
No guaranteed margin, savings, or approval
Professional review may be required for regulated work
Privacy: free-tier inputs processed in browser unless saved
